entry.title: NY Times: 10 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Jan. 6 Narrative Is Built on Trump, Proud Boys and Capitol Police
  • Jared and Ivanka, Without the Power or the Masks
  • Liz Cheney: Vice Chairwoman Has Become a G.O.P. Outcast for Condemning Trump
  • Michigan Governor Candidate Ryan Kelley, Who Was at the Capitol on Jan. 6, Is Arrested
  • Carl Paladino Calls Hitler ‘the Kind of Leader We Need,’ Drawing Backlash
  • New Documents Show Uvalde Police Waited to Confront Gunman
  • House Passes Red-Flag Bill as Senate Talks on Gun Violence Continue
  • Putin Compares Himself to Peter the Great, the Famous Czar
  • Michigan Police Officer Charged With Murder After Killing Black Motorist
  • Justice Dept. Is Investigating Louisiana Police Over Accusations of Abuse
  • The New Abortion Bans: Almost No Exceptions for Rape, Incest or Health
  • Logan Ury Says You’re Dating All Wrong
  • Island Records’ Chris Blackwell Finally Tells His Story
  • The Insurrection Didn’t End on Jan. 6. The Hearings Need to Prove That.
  • Abortion Didn’t Feel Like an Option. Neither Did Motherhood.
  • The Jan. 6 Committee Has Already Blown It
  • Getting Cars Off the Road Is Harder Than It Should Be
  • With Elon Musk, the Drama Is the Point
  • Liz Cheney Will Not Tolerate Trump’s Lies
  • Crime and Political Punishment
  • Russia and America Are Parting Ways
  • Your Thursday Evening Briefing
  • How a Highway Divided a Community in Philadelphia
  • The Proud Boys’ Path to Jan. 6
  • 人人都有枪真的会更安全吗？
  • Ukraine Live Updates: ‘Dead Cities’ Become the Focus of Fierce Fighting in the East
  • Summit Attendees Plan to Commit to Take in More Migrants
  • Chinese Pilots Sent a Message. American Allies Said They Went Too Far.
  • Iran Begins to Dismantle Nuclear Program Cameras After Western Criticism
  • The Racist Researcher Cited in the Buffalo Gunman’s Manifesto
  • Naftali Bennett, Israel’s Prime Minister, Visits U.A.E.
  • Saudi Arabia’s LIV Golf Series Upends Genteel World of Golf
  • At Least 7 Killed in Explosion and Fire in Daegu, South Korea
  • Russia Has Filled Ukrainian Water Canal to Crimea
  • Biden Faces Skepticism at Summit of the Americas
  • U.S. and Latin American Countries Will Commit to Receive More Migrants
  • Your Friday Briefing: A Crucial Moment in the Ukraine War
  • ‘Dead Cities’ Become the Flashpoint for the Fierce War in the East
  • Biden and Bolsonaro Meet for First Time at Summit
  • Foreign Fighters Sentenced to Death in Russia-Held Eastern Ukraine
  • Dmitry Kovtun, Accused Killer of Russian Dissident, Dies at 56
  • U.S. Accelerated Expulsions of Haitian Migrants in May
  • The Best Books to Read on Disinformation: Its History, Techniques and Effects
  • Russia Prosecuting 1,100 Captured Ukrainian Soldiers
  • Tourist Damages Rome’s Spanish Steps by Pushing a Scooter Down Them
  • German Brewers Face ‘Unprecedented’ Beer Bottle Shortage
  • 2 Officials in Vietnam Are Arrested in Virus Test Kit Scandal
  • Covid and Race
  • A Radical Collective Takes Over One of the World’s Biggest Art Shows
  • Republicans Try to Undercut Jan. 6 Committee With Inaccurate Claims
  • Why Lifeguards Are Scarce This Beach Season
  • What Tucker Carlson Said on Fox News During the Jan. 6 Hearing
  • Nick Quested: Documentary Filmmaker Who Embedded With Proud Boys Testifies at Hearing
  • Officer Caroline Edwards Testifies at Jan. 6 Hearing
  • Man Pleads Guilty to Selling $2.7 Million Worth of Unregistered Covid Pesticide
  • Jared Kushner’s Video Testimony Was Featured Prominently in Jan. 6 Hearing
  • Pete Aguilar: Top Democratic Messaging Official and Jan. 6 Committee Member
  • In Videotaped Testimony, Barr Dismissed Trump’s Claims of Fraud
  • Democrats Failed to Extend Assault Weapons Ban in 2004. They Regret It.
  • Who is Bennie Thompson?
  • Why the Jan 6. Committee Hired James Goldston to Produce the Hearings
  • Health Officials Warn About Impact to U.S. Coronavirus Test Supply Without Aid
  • Who Is Stephanie Murphy and What Is Her Role on the Jan. 6 Committee?
  • G.O.P. Lawmakers Who Went to D.C. on Jan. 6: Where Are They Now?
  • Read an Excerpt From Bennie Thompson’s Opening Statement
  • Who Is Zoe Lofgren?
  • House Again Delays Bill Bolstering Security for Supreme Court Justices
  • Employee Fatally Shoots 3 at Maryland Manufacturing Plant, Officials Say
  • Seeking a Return to Offices, Bosses Lost Leverage
  • Auto Safety Agency Expands Tesla Investigation
  • Disney Fires Peter Rice, Its Top TV Content Executive
  • Felicia Sonmez Is Fired by The Washington Post
  • Federal Prosecutors Open Criminal Inquiry of Wells Fargo’s Hiring Practices
  • The Billionaires Behind a Push to Reinvigorate U.S. Chip-Making
  • Median Rent in Manhattan Reaches a New High
  • Starbucks’s C.E.O. Howard Schultz: “I Don’t Know if We Can Keep Our Bathrooms Open”
  • Janet Yellen says the U.S. is likely to avoid a recession.
  • Sean Thackrey, Creator of Eccentric California Wines, Dies at 79
  • New 42 Worker Files Bias Lawsuit Over Diversity Training
  • Plastic Water Bottles to Be Phased Out at National Parks
  • Warner Bros. Discovery Picks Sports Chief to Navigate Streaming Era
  • Billionaires, Biden and Chips
  • The Grammys Have a New Award: Songwriter of the Year
  • Things to Do in Chicago: Food, Museums and More
  • Jump in Mortgage Rates Could Add $100,000 to Housing Costs
  • Biden Administration to Set Rules of the Road for Charging Electric Vehicles
  • ECB Prepares for First Interest Rate Increase in a Decade
  • It’s Easier Than Ever to Travel Abroad Without Cash
  • Fox News Doesn’t Plan to Carry Jan. 6 Hearings Live
  • Lina Khan, a Big Tech Critic, Tries Answering Her Own Detractors
  • You Won’t Use That Cool Feature
News from the New York Times

entry.title: Washington Post: 10 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Paula Rego, whose art captured ‘the beautiful grotesque,’ dies at 87
  • Got burping livestock? In New Zealand, you might be taxed.
  • Three foreign fighters sentenced to death as Russia lashes out
  • Russian forces pummel key cities as they advance in eastern Ukraine
  • Iran will remove 27 cameras from nuclear sites, U.N. watchdog says
  • How a sleeping 2-year-old and his dad won the Buffalo Marathon
  • Berlin car-ramming suspect shows signs of mental illness, police say
  • Separatist court gives death sentences to Britons, Moroccan who fought for Ukraine
  • Thailand legalizes marijuana — with gray areas and caveats
  • Belgian king offers ‘regrets’ but no apology for colonialism in Congo
  • A deadly disease is wiping out Caribbean coral, researchers find
  • WHO covid origins report says ‘lab leak’ theory needs further investigation
  • Microplastics found in fresh Antarctic snow, as pollutants spread
  • Swirling doubts herald major shifts at upcoming Chinese political meeting
  • Who are the Gupta brothers, the family accused of looting South Africa?
  • Hacked Russian radio station broadcasts Ukrainian anthem
  • U.N. seeks deal on food exports as war drives up prices and hurts millions worldwide
  • Ukraine, coping with war losses, adds new polish to military funerals
  • Why a 1970s-style ‘stagflation’ crisis would hit the world’s poor hard
  • UN elects new council members including Japan, Switzerland
  • Iran pulls UN nuke cameras in possible 'fatal blow' to deal
  • Chinese fighter jet crashes into homes, killing 1 on ground
  • Search narrows, pressure mounts in Amazon disappearance
  • Migrants split on whether to keep walking through Mexico
  • Niacaragua authorizes entry of Russian troops, planes, ships
  • Grand Rapids officer who killed Patrick Lyoya charged with second-degree murder
  • March for Our Lives returns to D.C. to rally for gun control
  • Five Marines killed in Osprey crash in California desert
  • She got an STD during car sex. Now, Geico could pay her $5.2 million.
  • Teen beaten to death outside school funded by LeBron James foundation
  • Parents of trans children sue Texas to stop child-abuse investigations
  • Social agitators, joyfully Black: The artistic heirs of Gordon Parks
  • Police say brothers — 11 and 14 — broke into gun store, stole 22 weapons
  • Jan. 6 hearing recap: Police officer describes bloody ‘war scene’ outside Capitol
  • Jan. 6 committee uses video, testimony to tell tale of the insurrection
  • Jan. 6 panel to hear from Capitol Police officer, filmmaker as high-drama hearings begin
  • Mich. gubernatorial candidate arrested on Jan. 6 Capitol riot charge
  • D.C. chiropractor being sued in officer’s assault is charged in Jan. 6 riot
  • Explosion at Texas LNG plant puts added strain on global energy market
  • Jewelry giant settles gender-discrimination lawsuit for $175 million
  • Mortgage rates move higher ahead of Federal Reserve meeting
  • Most Americans expect inflation to get worse, Post-Schar School poll finds
  • My co-worker is asking to borrow my clothes
  • Summer Game Fest 2022: Main event reveals Last of Us updates, new game announcements, ‘Street Fighter 6’ and ‘Modern Warfare 2’ trailers
  • Amazon’s request to close hearing on union victory to public is denied
  • NASA inspector general issues a scathing report on moon effort
  • NASA joins the hunt for UFOs
  • Maxed-out townhouse sets the stage for minimal impact on environment
  • Why foundation cracks in your home shouldn’t be ignored
  • China’s Big Problem That Xi Jinping Can’t Solve
  • That Chevy Feels A Lot Better Than a Tesla Right Now
  • India’s Central Bank Is Getting Its Way on Rates. But For How Long?
  • It’s Time to Get Biofuels Out of Your Gas Tank
  • Insurrection? Sedition? Incitement? A Legal Guide to the Capitol Riot
  • Meet the Guptas, Symbols of South African Corruption
  • How Putin’s Spooking Japan Further Away From Pacifism
  • How Old-Style Buy Now, Pay Later Became Trendy ‘BNPL’
  • Tune out the ‘it won’t matter’ coverage of the Jan. 6 hearings
  • The Kavanaugh threat exposed weaknesses in judicial security — and our discourse
  • DeSantis attacks the Rays — and free speech
  • The Jan. 6 committee may have bruised Trump, but he’s still standing
  • Trump will be gone, but the GOP’s ‘dishonor will remain’
  • The best China strategy? Defeat Russia.
  • Democrats can’t blame Trump for Jan. 6 but absolve Schumer for threatening Kavanaugh
  • The ‘next’ Jan. 6 is happening, and the Supreme Court is the target
  • Putin’s Ukraine war draws on his vision of nationalism, exceptionalism
  • No, Republicans, prairie dogs don’t matter more than murdered children.
  • Ohio Republicans’ proposal to arm teachers is an act of cowardice
  • As our entitlements crisis gets closer, a solution moved farther away
  • Americans deserve to know if ATF will continue ‘stash house stings’
  • How did the pandemic begin? China must help find the answer.
  • 6 takeaways from the Jan. 6 committee’s powerful case against Trump
  • Could Congress really act (gasp!) to protect democracy?
  • How NIMBYism chokes off affordable housing even in Big Sky Country
  • Out of touch with reality
  • A political crisis exposes the fictions of the West Bank settlement enterprise
  • My attacker has been jailed. But who was pulling the strings?
  • Not a bridge to nowhere, but not a sight to behold
  • Susan Collins is nowhere to be found when we most need her
  • Ineffective gun reform should not be the goal
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 10 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Johnny Depp and musician Jeff Beck announce new joint album, 18
  • Blind backs 'control freak' Ten Hag to deliver at Manchester United
  • India's retail inflation likely slipped only modestly in May - Reuters poll
  • US Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin and Ng Eng Hen affirm need for stable defence relations
  • Striking S Korean truckers' union: To hold 2nd round of talks with transport ministry
  • Striking S Korean truckers plan to stop shipments of raw materials for chips
  • Asian stocks track global shares lower, US CPI in focus
  • Oil falls on demand worries over Shanghai's new partial lockdowns
  • Indonesia raises maximum palm oil export tax -regulation document
  • China's producer inflation eases to 14-month low in May
  • Japan business community seeks further relaxation of border controls
  • Dollar on front foot as traders await US inflation data for Fed cues
  • Wildfire in southern Spain forces town evacuation; 3 hurt
  • Japan Finance Minister Suzuki dodges question of possible FX intervention
  • Defence says missile theory unproven as MH17 trial wraps
  • Foreign fighters in Ukraine sentenced to death by pro-Russians
  • Stay on the go by keeping your natural defences strong

  • Britney Spears' ex-husband crashes California wedding site
  • Shanghai faces unexpected round of COVID-19 testing for most residents
  • Two COVID-19 Omicron subvariants detected in Malaysia

  • Brazil police probe ties to illegal fishing in case of missing British journalist
  • US Capitol riot hearings to put focus on Trump aides' words
  • After veto on North Korea, China says 'let's see' on UN action over a nuclear test
  • US approves US$120 million sale to maintain Taiwanese warships
  • UN calls for justice after fatal shooting of Myanmar WHO worker
  • Top US official meets Philippines' Marcos Jr to boost 'longstanding alliance'
  • Bon Odori is a cultural festival, says Selangor sultan after minister tells Muslims to avoid the event
  • Thai cops seize meth hidden in replica Easter Island statue

  • Japan Prime Minister Fumio Kishida to meet President Halimah, PM Lee during official visit to Singapore

  • Supply of chickens in Malaysia expected to stabilise from June, says agriculture minister

  • In Nepal's capital, piles of garbage put off tourists and residents
  • Thailand legalises growing, consumption of marijuana
  • Record surge in Japan's wholesale import costs rekindles weak yen concerns

  • S. Korea's truckers' strike hits output at Hyundai's biggest auto plants
  • Temporary administrator takes over China Moly's Tenke mine in Congo
  • South Korea April current account logs first deficit in 2 years
  • AMD flags slow market for PCs in 2022
  • 'Nothing to suggest' US will have a recession: Yellen
  • Commentary: It's still near impossible to get a Sony Playstation 5
  • US SEC opens probe against Ericsson over 2019 Iraq bribery report
  • Stitch Fix to cut workforce by 15% of salaried roles
  • Heartbroken to see her kids’ bloody scratches from eczema, this mum started a business to help them
  • Commentary: Cabinet reshuffle shows how leaders are assembling around Lawrence Wong for complex tasks ahead
  • Man to be charged after allegedly cheating local banks, enabling criminals to launder proceeds from love scams
  • 39-year-old man arrested for drink driving after accident on Nicoll Highway involving 3 cyclists
  • Two professors given Honorary Citizen Award for their contributions to Singapore
  • 8 people arrested after more than S$60,000 lost in DBS SMS phishing scams
  • Property agent gets jail for deceiving buyer and seller of HDB flat to receive double commission
  • Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to deliver virtual address at Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore
  • PM Lee Hsien Loong congratulates Australia PM Anthony Albanese on his appointment
  • 'Overwhelming response' for StarHub's Premier League package, promo offer available to all until Jun 19, says telco
  • Tanjong Pagar car crash: Driver was drink-driving and speeding before fatal collision
  • Singapore introduces framework for sovereign green bonds ahead of inaugural issuance
  • Public advised against using Star Cream for diaper rash; baby hospitalised due to steroid toxicity
  • COVID rules NZ skipper Williamson out of second England test
  • Egypt suffer shock loss to Ethiopia as Cameroon and Morocco win
  • Norway held to home stalemate by 10-man Slovenia
  • Jovic goal gives Serbia win over lacklustre Sweden
  • Sarabia strikes early as Spain win in Switzerland
  • Cancelo, Guedes strike as Portugal ease past Czech Republic
  • Jackson steals the show with 200m victory in Rome
  • PGA Tour players have no sympathy for suspended LIV deserters
  • Hartley secures sixth successive pole for Toyota at Le Mans
  • Mickelson plays it straight as PGA Tour suspends him
  • Garcia 'not bothered' by PGA Tour suspension
  • Salah, Kerr win PFA Player of the Year awards
  • Hamilton gets honorary Brazilian citizenship
  • US 16-year-old Pierson ready for record Le Mans debut

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 10 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 10 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-10 03:30AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Law stopping Hong Kong bosses from raiding MPF funds to cover staff payment obligations is too little, too late, critics argue
  • Two shots fired in gang clash near Hong Kong’s Lan Kwai Fong nightlife hub, as knife-wielding attackers ambush group
  • Why Hong Kong has changed its Covid-19 case reporting criteria, and how this affects you
  • Jumbo Floating Restaurant saga another sign of Hong Kong’s inability to preserve its contemporary history
  • Former employee of scandal-hit Hong Kong child protection group pleads guilty to abuse, first of 27 suspects to do so
  • Hong Kong bosses should be flexible with workers caring for elderly or disabled residents at home, study says
  • Owner of domestic helper agency in Hong Kong denies wrongdoing over death of worker
  • Hong Kong heritage advisers urge officials to work with advocates to reassess value of pre-war building once used for spying against Japanese during World War II
  • 8 Labrador puppies bred to detect drugs, explosives under programme between customs, Hong Kong airport’s security company
  • New training school for Hong Kong government workers to focus on raising awareness of national security, college head says
  • Hong Kong police arrest 46 in crackdown on syndicate using modem-pool devices in new tactic to harass debtors into paying up
  • Japan reopens to visitors on June 10, but Hong Kong travellers may have to delay departure, with visa applications up in the air
  • Credit card company sues wife of former Hong Kong leader CY Leung for failing to pay HK$90,000 bill
  • Hong Kong teenagers arrested, 10.5kg of ketamine seized during crackdown on drug syndicate
  • Hong Kong leader rules out reopening border with mainland China any time soon, pointing to nation’s tough Covid-19 policy
  • Hong Kong invites global financiers to two-day November summit in much-heralded bid to reclaim city’s spot in world finance
  • 2 jobless Hong Kong men jailed 10 years for causing woman’s death in snatch theft, judge calls crime a ‘cowardly attack’
  • Body of missing Hong Kong hiker, 52, found a day after she was swept away while swimming in country park stream
  • Hong Kong passes long-awaited labour bill to scrap MPF offsetting mechanism, protecting workers’ pensions
  • Early years matters
  • China inflation: consumer costs remain stable as factory-gate prices ease further
  • Earth’s inner secrets revealed in oldest rocks found on ocean floor
  • US is so bent on resisting China’s rise, it has ignored its own decline
  • Chinese game developer denies ‘pay to work’ offer after job post sparks controversy amid tough job market
  • Coronavirus: China moves to calm foreign investors after ‘unprecedented’ shock of Shanghai lockdown
  • US is facing supply chain challenges, but so is China, experts say
  • US taking aim at industrial labour shortfall and China supply dependency, Pentagon official says
  • How much is China’s foreign direct investment and is it still a good destination for overseas investors?
  • In the eyes of others, the US is not the benign power it thinks it is
  • WHO says Covid-19 origins unclear but lab leak theory needs study
  • China-Germany economic ties shift with Xinjiang allegations, zero Covid and supply chain issues
  • Inflation isn’t China’s problem, income is: ‘all we can do is spend less’
  • China’s chip import volume declines in first five months as rigid Covid-19 control measures disrupt manufacturing sector
  • Beijing hits out at Washington over proposed US$120 million arms deal to Taiwan
  • Taiwan is installing Bee Eye radars on its outlying islands to track PLA buzzing
  • Ant Group, China securities regulator deny reports Alibaba’s fintech giant is reviving stalled IPO
  • European Parliament passes landslide vote on China’s alleged rights abuses in Xinjiang
  • Chinese state broadcasting gets a new chief: Xi protégé Xu Lin
  • Chinese court awards woman US$4,500 compensation for 7 years of housework and child-rearing from ex-husband after divorce
  • China digital currency: e-CNY ‘well positioned’ but still ‘a way to go’ to challenge US dollar dominance
  • Xiaohongshu, dubbed China’s Instagram, tests Web3 waters with investment in US start-up Shil.me
  • Xi Jinping tells officials to ensure economic and social stability ahead of Communist Party congress
  • Shanghai moves towards ‘watershed moment’ with Disney resort reopening partially; city reports six new Covid-19 cases
  • Alibaba, Meituan paid the bulk of China’s US$3 billion antitrust fines in 2021, report shows
  • Malaysia Airlines flight MH17: Defence says missile theory unproven as trial wraps
  • New Zealand’s Jacinda Ardern says collective effort needed to rebuff China’s advances in Pacific
  • Amid violent unrest, Myanmar’s military junta hopes to revive tourism
  • Japan’s Kishida to send China a warning on Taiwan at Singapore’s Shangri-La Dialogue, analysts say
  • Why won’t Asean condemn Myanmar junta’s plan to hang four people? In case they need to use the same option
  • Stinking piles of rubbish are damaging tourism and locals are ‘forced to live like pigs’ in Nepal’s capital city
  • Robert Kuok still tops Malaysia’s rich list with US$11 billion as wealth of country’s tycoons tumbles 10 per cent
  • China and Singapore to resume military exercises, cooperate on defence education
  • Philippines billionaire Razon plans massive solar panel facility
  • Sri Lanka president’s brother, Basil Rajapaksa, resigns from parliament
  • How BJP’s Nupur Sharma’s Prophet Mohammed comments pushed India into diplomatic maelstrom
  • Two Chinese men arrested over alleged plot to cheat on Japan university exam
  • North Korea’s record missile flurry could have cost as much as US$650 million
  • Coronavirus: Japan has dropped its outdoor mask mandate, but the message has not filtered through to the public
  • South Korea office building fire kills at least 7, arson suspected
  • US Capitol riot hearing shows Trump allies, daughter Ivanka rejected election fraud claims
  • Three killed, officer wounded in Maryland shooting
  • Now there’s a Sriracha shortage, and spice lovers are up in arms
  • Nasa joins hunt for UFOs, hiring experts to solve ‘perplexing mysteries’
  • Russia making more money off oil and gas than before Ukraine war, US official says
  • Slain Texas couple’s missing baby girl found in Oklahoma 42 years later
  • Vladimir Putin compares Ukraine actions with Peter the Great’s conquests
  • Covid during pregnancy doubled babies’ risk of delayed development in speech, motor skills
  • Convicted R&B singer R Kelly should get more than 25 years in prison, say prosecutors
  • Three foreigners who fought for Ukraine sentenced to death by firing squad
  • Battle for Sievierodonetsk ‘brutal’, Zelenksy says as Russia pushes Ukrainian troops to city’s outskirts
  • Russia attacking Ukraine food targets to scare world: governor
  • Russia cracks down on critics of its ‘military operation’ in Ukraine
  • New US nuclear-missile submarines hobbled by billions in growing costs and delays
  • Teslas with Autopilot are closer to recall amid elevated investigation into collisions in US
  • TSMC’s US$100 billion wipeout does not change analysts’ bullish outlook for Taiwan’s semiconductor giant
  • Alibaba leads tech slide in Hong Kong as China, Ant Group deny IPO revival plans while Nio sinks after EV forecasts underwhelm
  • Delisting risk, souring relations are hurdles for Chinese biotech firms seeking to enter US market, analyst says
  • In solving real-world needs, decentralised finance can only continue to grow
  • Electric cars: Nio warns of fewer deliveries this quarter as anti-pandemic curbs hit production, weaken sales
  • Alibaba’s cloud services unit jumps on NFT bandwagon amid a global market downturn for these digital assets
  • Family offices’ investments in China expected to cool down amid regulatory crackdown, real estate stress, UBS survey finds
  • Buffett-backed BYD boosts price ceiling by 33 per cent for stock buy-back plan in vote of confidence on market outlook
  • Why the yuan isn’t a big winner in shift away from US dollar
  • New World Development’s Humansa expands footprint in health and wellness in Greater Bay Area with acquisition and partnership
  • Ant Group’s latest Singapore expansion brings mobile payment apps from Malaysia and South Korea to the city
  • Genshin Impact developer miHoYo gets a boost as Beijing grants game licence for expected shooting game
  • Netflix K-drama Welcome to Wedding Hell lays bare the troubles in 2 becoming 1
  • Who is Julia Garner, actress picked to play Madonna in biopic? Her starring roles in Ozark and Inventing Anna on Netflix
  • How to make veal tartare with pickled shallot and Parmesan-paprika palmiers – a fancy dinner party starter that’s lean, light and elegant
  • Disney+ K-drama Link: Eat, Love, Kill – Yeo Jin-goo, Moon Ga-young make a compelling pair in engaging fantasy comedy-drama-mystery
  • New art fair Tokyo Gendai will introduce international contemporary artists to a younger generation of Japanese collectors
  • When Thai food meets Sichuan: Hong Kong chef talks spices, mixing the two cuisines and TCM food at new restaurant Sawadee Chef
  • Whatever happened to Kate Bush, singer of Stranger Things hit song Running Up That Hill? From her US$60 million net worth and reclusive lifestyle, to her 1985 hit making a chart comeback
  • Inside Johnny Depp’s OTT US$100 million property portfolio: from the Bahamas island where he married Amber Heard, to a French village he bought Vanessa Paradis and a Kentucky farm named after his mum
  • Who is Harry Styles’ girlfriend Olivia Wilde, really? The Tron actress based her stage name on Oscar Wilde, has two kids with Ted Lasso star Jason Sudeikis, and was once even married to a prince
  • Who are BTS’ celebrity pals in Hollywood? The K-pop idols hung with Coldplay’s Chris Martin after meeting Joe Biden at the White House, and are close with Lizzo, Halsey and Megan Thee Stallion too
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 10 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • January 6 riot was part of ‘attempted coup’ by Trump, committee alleges
  • Japan’s edict throws tourism reopening into chaos and confusion
  • Big Tech tiptoes to the metaverse
  • PGA Tour suspends 17 golfers for joining Saudi rival LIV
  • Live news updates: Shanghai to re-shutter half of city districts after community cases emerge
  • ECB takes hawkish turn to counter record-high inflation
  • Russian-backed court sentences UK and Moroccan ‘mercenaries’ to death
  • Ordinary Ukrainians wage war with digital tools and drones
  • Disney sacks top TV executive as board rallies around Chapek
  • Disney pads up for title defence in $5bn Indian cricket battle
  • Spac boom dies as wary investors retreat
  • Canada’s oil sands: why some of the world’s dirtiest fuel is now in hot demand
  • US energy envoy asks India to restrain Russian oil purchases
  • European gas prices surge after fire at Texas LNG plant
  • State Street knocks down Credit Suisse takeover rumours
  • Citi suffered tech glitch during height of Covid market stress
  • DWS chief in line for millions of euros in severance pay
  • Sunak blamed for losing £11bn in servicing UK government debt
  • Johnson faces Eurosceptic revolt over Northern Ireland protocol bill
  • US stocks weaken ahead of inflation print
  • Iran to remove 27 cameras from nuclear facilities
  • Deloitte denies it failed to protect former female employee from harassment
  • Ambani’s Reliance teams up with Apollo to make binding bid for Boots
  • Singapore’s wealthy pursue luxury cars even as inflation drives up prices
  • Khan vows ‘muscular’ US antitrust approach on private equity deals
  • ‘Super deductor’ tax break fails to boost UK business investment
  • The UK’s credit gap will gape in this cost of living crisis
  • Plans to curb corporate crime put forward to UK government
  • News updates from the January 6 hearing: Senior DoJ leaders threatened mass resignation to prevent Trump overturning election, Cheney’s opening presentations stand out and Proud Boys and Oath Keepers play central role
  • FirstFT: China preps for permanent zero-Covid
  • Unison warns of public-sector strikes unless pay deals match cost of living
  • Johnson proposes allowing housing benefits to pay mortgage
  • Centrica applies to reopen Britain’s biggest gas storage site
  • Food stocks: price rises keep inflationary pressure at bay, for now
  • Airlines have made promises they are unable to deliver
  • City economist picked to be lord mayor of London in 2023
  • The global race for supercomputing power
  • Why maximalist Northern Ireland demands won’t work
  • Britain under Johnson has abandoned ideals of political leadership

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
